channel.title: BinExp on Tzion
channel.description: Recent content in BinExp on Tzion

item.title: [BOH] format2win
item.description: This challenge provided a binary file. Since this is a pwn challenge, before we dig into it, let’s see its security properties: From result we can see that there is no PIE enabled and no stack canary. Position Independent Executables (PIE) is a security measure that protect the binary and all of its dependencies by loading it into random locations within virtual memory each time the application is executed. Stack canaries or security cookies are tell-tale values added to binaries during compilation to protect critical stack values like the Return Pointer against buffer overflow attacks.

item.title: [NAHAMCONCTF] Babysteps
item.description: Task source: https://github.com/Tzion0/CTF/tree/master/NahamConCTF/2022/Binary_Exploitation/Babysteps Description Become a baby! Take your first steps and jump around with BABY SIMULATOR 9000! This challenge provided a C source code and its binary. The C source code: #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <sys/stat.h> #define BABYBUFFER 16 void setup(void) { setbuf(stdout, NULL); setbuf(stdin, NULL); } void whine() { puts("You whine: 'WAAAAAAHHHH!! WAAH, WAAHH, WAAAAAAHHHH'\n"); } void scream() { puts("You scream: 'WAAAAAAHHHH!! WAAH, WAAHH, WAAAAAAHHHH'\n"); } void cry() { puts("You cry: 'WAAAAAAHHHH!

item.title: [PICOCTF] Binary Exploitation
item.description: Tasks source: https://github.com/Tzion0/CTF/tree/master/PicoCTF/2022/Binary_Exploitation Worth to note that we managed to get rank 468 out of 7794 teams in PicoCTF 2022. This writeup contains 10 out of 14 Binary Exploitation category challenges in PicoCTF 2022 that i solved. basic-file-exploit Description The program provided allows you to write to a file and read what you wrote from it. Try playing around with it and see if you can break it! This challenge provided a C source code:
